compiled_state.title: As a maintainer, I can run scripts/release.sh to cut a release with safety preconditions, but without yet altering client channel resolution
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a new scripts/release.sh at the repo root that cuts a release only after CI green + clean tree + on main up-to-date with origin/main + plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci passes. Add INSTALL.md documentation describing both channels (dso, dso-dev), recommending auto-update via the /plugin UI, and stating version semantics. The script exists and is testable; it does not yet alter client channel resolution.

**Why**: Releases must be deliberate, gated, and auditable. The maintainer-facing release tool plus consumer-facing distribution docs are the operational mechanism that makes the two-channel model usable in practice.

**Scope**:
- IN: scripts/release.sh at repo root (executable, ~80-150 lines bash); 4 preconditions checked with clear abort messages; tag-uniqueness check before tag creation; marketplace.json post-edit JSON validation; explicit interactive confirmation (or --yes flag) before the push step; INSTALL.md rewritten to document both channels, install commands, auto-update guidance, and version semantics.
- OUT: Actually cutting v1.13.0 (Story 3); CLAUDE.md / ADR updates (Story 4); restructuring marketplace.json (Story 1, prerequisite).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, scripts/release.sh exists at repo root, is executable, and aborts with a clear error message naming the failed precondition when (a) CI is not green on the commit being tagged, (b) the working tree is dirty or the branch is not main / not up-to-date with origin/main, or (c) plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci fails.
  ← Satisfies: \"scripts/release.sh cuts a release only after confirming (a) CI is green on the commit being tagged, (b) the working tree is clean and the local branch is main up-to-date with origin/main, and (c) plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci passes; the script aborts with a clear error message naming the failed precondition otherwise\"
- When this story is complete, scripts/release.sh validates the marketplace.json edit produces valid JSON before committing, and aborts if the result fails python3 -m json.tool.
  ← Satisfies: \"scripts/release.sh cuts a release only after confirming...the script aborts with a clear error message naming the failed precondition otherwise\"
- When this story is complete, scripts/release.sh requires explicit interactive confirmation (or a --yes flag) before pushing to origin/main.
  ← Satisfies: \"scripts/release.sh cuts a release only after confirming...the script aborts...\"
- When this story is complete, INSTALL.md documents (a) the install command for dso (stable channel), (b) the install command for dso-dev (dev channel), (c) recommendation to enable auto-update on the marketplace via the /plugin UI, and (d) a one-line statement of version semantics (dso advances on tagged releases; dso-dev advances on every merge to main).
  ← Satisfies: \"INSTALL.md documents both channels (install commands for dso and dso-dev, recommendation to enable auto-update on the marketplace via the /plugin UI, and a one-line statement of the version semantics — dso advances on tagged releases, dso-dev advances on every merge to main)\"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for release.sh precondition logic (mocked git/CI outputs verifying each precondition fires its abort message and exits non-zero).

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Script writes to git and pushes to origin/main — must be idempotent on partial failure; tag-uniqueness check before creating.
- [Testing] Preconditions should be designed as pure functions (or testable shell functions with mocked git/curl) to avoid requiring a live CI/git round-trip in unit tests.
- [Security] Push privileges to main — require interactive confirmation or explicit --yes flag before push.
- [Maintainability] Per CLAUDE.md rule 26, scripts/release.sh lives at repo root, NOT inside plugins/dso/. It is a dev-team artifact, not part of the distributed plugin.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
